title: papaver dubium f. ambiguum
intro: Papaver dubium f. ambiguum, also known as the Yellow Poppy or False Meconopsis, is a flowering plant that is part of the Poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Papaver dubium f. ambiguum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as an expectorant to help relieve coughs and congestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its flowers and leaves can be used to make medicinal preparations.
